oneOf.description: A section heading at levels 1–6. Supports inline text runs with optional typography overrides and automatic table-of-contents inclusion.
A block of flowing text composed of one or more inline text runs. Supports per-block typography and optional first-line indent.
An embedded image from a URL, file path, or base-64 data URI. Width and height are in mm; omit either to auto-size. Never split across pages by default.
A table of rows and cells with optional header rows, column widths (mm), borders, and a caption. Header rows repeat on every page by default. Never split across pages by default.
A bulleted or numbered list with unlimited nesting depth. Set ordered to true for a numbered list and start to change the initial counter value.
A preformatted code block rendered in a monospace font. Optionally specify the language identifier for syntax highlighting and enable line numbers. Never split across pages by default.
A pull-quote or cited excerpt displayed with a left accent bar. Optional attribution line rendered below the quote text. Never split across pages by default.
A horizontal rule used to visually separate sections. Width is expressed as a percentage of the available content area (default 100%).
An invisible vertical gap of a fixed height (mm). Use to add whitespace between blocks without relying on margins.
An explicit page break. All content following this block starts at the top of the next page.
